item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made them spheres because Dave's original was. I am now very tempted to change them to octohedrons though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm not sure why, but I always imagine these as octahedrons.
Oh hey, this passed the first century, it seems.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this. I kinda wish I could read the original though out of curiosity. Still, it's a fun read and really adds onto my headcanon.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, you referenced my skip! I love this article and I can't believe I just now saw it. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCP-2004 has a similar theme. There's probably room for more approaches to it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm slightly upset because by unfortunate coincidence I had an idea very similar to this a while ago I wanted to use, but since you've executed this far better than I ever could, I don't care that I had bad luck. Nice work. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah has been for a while now. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh hey, it made it up. Kudos.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm getting tired of the "IT CAME FROM OUTER SPACE" type SCPs. However, with that being said, this was nevertheless very well executed and earns an up-vote for being well written and well thought out.

item.title: Math Error
item.content:encoded: I am not sure where I got some of those numbers. I recalculated and made some changes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I just assumed it meant whoever is in charge of this has a connection to SCP-1342

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Should be 99-Tripurantaka? CRUCIFORM SNARE is the codename of the satellites.

Yeah, but that is the best one of those. I upvoted that page on yours alone. It is going to be a hard act to follow…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Also, just letting you know, I did use this. Under SCP-2123. Operation Cruciform Snare worked, but mainly because there was extra anti-matter everywhere.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know, it has a whole Golden Age of Science Fiction thing going on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It was totally worth rewriting to save that phrase. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this. How about we consider 2631 and my own 2004 to be part of the same little canon? Then no one's copying anyone, just writing in the same shared world.

item.title: Re: Tags
item.content:encoded: Also in item five of 99-Tripurantaka.

item.title: Re: Tags
item.content:encoded: That might have gotten overlooked since it was in the footnotes. Added. Good catch.

item.title: Tags
item.content:encoded:
I noticed the tag team got here, but shouldn't this also have a k-class scenario tag (this article mentions two possible XK events and one NK event)?

Edit: Should it also have self-replicating and sapient?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Part of me wants to brush this off for relying too heavily on its crosslinks,

Originally the 2001 crossslink was blackbox, but I think it works better this way (and it is buried fairly deep in the article). I am not sure what other crossslinks you mean…

Let's go with +1. That'll do nicely.

Thank you!

Also, I just looked up Tripurantaka. Ooh. Well played.

Yes, I though so too. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm conflicted about this article. Part of me wants to brush this off for relying too heavily on its crosslinks, but the other parts are pointing out how delightfully eerie the overall effect was. In the end, they would add, I did quite enjoy it.

Let's go with +1. That'll do nicely.

edit: Also, I just looked up Tripurantaka. Ooh. Well played.

item.title: Wherein I Post Lengthy Spoilery Analysis
item.content:encoded:
Let us assume that the following are true:

  • The preconditions for a 99-Tripurantaka event is a Keter level object at the verge of a K-Class scenario with potential interstellar consequences.
  • The array's first response is containment either via nanobots or something more esoteric (like the 1987 expirement).
  • The planet cracker relativistic impact is a worst case destruction failsafe.

The purpose of 99-Tripurantaka is both to neutralize SCP-2631 and prevent the triggering k-class event.

The following scenarios may occur:

+ Lengthy spoilers
– hide block

Case I

99-Tripurantaka: Not Deployed
Array Containment:Deployed/Fails
Array Destruct: Deployed/Success
Triggering Keter: Survives/Escapes
Result: Planetary XK event caused by relativistic impact. Keter Object is freed for larger scale K-Class event.
Notes: Worst case scenario.

Case II

99-Tripurantaka: Not Deployed
Array Containment:Deployed/Fails
Array Destruct: Deployed/Fails
Triggering Keter: Survives/Escapes
Result: Planetary XK event followed by larger scale K-Class scenario, both caused by Keter object.
Notes: Net result identical to Case I.

Case III

99-Tripurantaka: Not Deployed
Array Containment:Deployed/Fails
Array Destruct: Deployed/Success
Triggering Keter: Destroyed
Result: Planetary XK event caused by relativistic impact.
Notes: We are extinct but at least the galaxy is safe.

Case IV

99-Tripurantaka: Not Deployed
Array Containment:Deployed/Succeeds
Array Destruct: Not Deployed
Triggering Keter: Contained by Array
Result: Potential catastrophic restructuring or extinction of human civilization.
Notes: May be survivable but this is unknowable. In an eariler draft I had a memo discussing treating this as a potential Thaumiel. Eskobar pointed out this is like a plan to have a bully punch you in the face to kill a spider on your nose.

Case V

99-Tripurantaka: Deployed/Fails
Array Containment:Deployed/ As Cases I-IV.
Array Destruct: As Cases I-IV.
Triggering Keter: As Cases I-IV.
Result: As Cases I-IV.
Notes: As Cases I-IV. Given the vast gulf in technology and resources this is probably the most likely outcome of a 99-Tripurantaka deployment.

Case VI

99-Tripurantaka: Deployed/Succeeds only in destroying the array.
Array Containment: Destroyed by Foundation
Array Destruct: Deployed/As Cases I-III.
Triggering Keter: As Cases I-III.
Result: As Cases I-III.
Notes: As Cases I-III. All outcomes result in human extinction.

Case VII

99-Tripurantaka: Deployed/Succeeds both in destroying the array, and containing/destroying/neutralizing the Keter.
Array Containment: Destroyed by Foundation
Array Destruct: Not Deployed or Deployed/Fails
Triggering Keter: Contained (or Neutralized/Destroyed) by human action.
Result: Humanity survives with mitigated damage.
Notes: Probable best case post-deployment scenario. This is the one that communicating with the other organizations helps the most with.

Case VIII

99-Tripurantaka: Deployed/Succeeds both in destroying the array, and containing/destroying/neutralizing the Keter.
Array Containment: Destroyed by Foundation
Array Destruct: Deployed/Succeeds
Triggering Keter: Contained (or Neutralized/Destroyed) by human action.
Result: XK scenario due to relativistic impact.
Notes: Ironic extinction event. This could result from the aliens being unable to recall the impactor after destruction of the array or from punishing us for destroying it. Or just too-late containment after the point of no return.

Case IX

99-Tripurantaka: Not Deployed or Deployed/Fails
Array Containment:Deployed/Fails or only partially succeeds.
Array Destruct: Deployed/Fails
Triggering Keter: Causes less than full scale event.
Result: Human civilization badly damaged but species survives.
Notes: Post apocalypse scenario. This one also benefits from warning the other organizations. Least likely scenario as the impactor probably won't just miss.

Did I miss any major permutations?

As you can see, if it gets to this point there really aren't any good options. Much better to control the Keter before anything else happens.

Of course if any of the initial assumptions about the aliens motives or capabilities are wrong, then this whole line of reasoning may be fatally misguided.

– hide block

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, repurposing the original title as a codename worked really well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Daveyoufool actually came up with the phrase and sirpudding was given permission to adopt it. Either way it is a very cool phrase :P
I had an idea similar to this but could never figure out how to make it work. Kudos for pulling it off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ah, so our whole planet is being contained. Interesting. Honestly, the best part of this is the containment procedures; I like the sense of "we are really fucked if this happens". Also, "cruciform snare" is just a great phrase.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, I hadn't really thought about that for the second point. Neat!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
1. They might have the object that's causing the fuss.
  2. They might know what object is causing the fuss.
  3. They might be causing the fuss.
  4. They have an interest in not being on an exploding planet.

Edit: I answer in more detail below (see Case VII and IX).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would think if shit is real, even your enemies can become your allies. In fact, that was where I made up my mind and upvoted. The sci-fi was commendable, but that small remark about CI brought something new to the table and I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Quick question, why alert the Chaos Insurgency? aren't they antagonistic to the Foundation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thank you, and no problem, you were a big help!

I do really want to do some really complicated layered weird skip eventually, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks! Yeah those it's/its are pesky. Looks like Crayne killed a Shark Punching Center for me too (and I did a search-replace for those).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am a big partisan for emergent systems and in general for fuzzier categories. I don't think humans are especially unique even on Earth, we just put some different things together that other animals don't in the same way. I think life is probably pretty unique though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Compared to some of the drafts I read, this is fantastic. Like I said, simplicity is your friend. This is probably the shortest SCP to unnerve me for a long time, and I'm sorry I didn't give concrit on the later drafts.

Minor suggestion: put an actual crosslink to SCP-2001 for ease of finding it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That's probably a more realistic interpretation, but I'm something of a romantic.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
(Fixed an it's/its grammar issue)

…I can use this. It's just adding to the theme of all sorts of alien crafts treating the Earth as a Very Bad Thing.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Also, the implication that humans are the only species to make music is nice.

That's one of the "sacred cows" that dave asked me to retain. Personally I am not a fan of human uniqueness. Instead I like to think that "music-maker" means a entire classification of non-tentacle-horror biological sapients, but I left it open to interpretation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like this version much better than the original. (sorry, dave) The hard SF elements really sell it much better and the partially translated note brings it all together. Also, the implication that humans are the only species to make music is nice.

Oh, and extra +1 for using the logical extension of the AIR-2 Genie. Nothing says love like a single rocket designed to take out an entire fleet of bombers.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can probably elucidate if you need it.

+ The main theme.
- The main  theme.

Aliens are treating the Earth the way the Foundation treats a contained Keter anomaly.


Any specific questions?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I know, for certain, that I have no idea what's going on in this article. Like not a bit. But I actually really like it. +1, bud.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Spoilers on spoilers
- Put it back in the failsafe Keter safe Safe safe

I wonder then, if the Foundation is truly convinced it won't work

I think it's more of a Hail Mary. Maybe the nukes will kill the satellites and the nanomachine payloads. If we are really lucky, just maybe they'll miss the Earth with their impactor. Worth a shot. I also wanted to imply a bit of an Apollyon here, if we have a plan at least we won't be freaking out about not having a plan. Right?

the Keter skips that are to be "unsealed" are just to get things over with as quickly as possible,

Actually my thought was that these are all destruction, neutralization, or K-class mitigation orders (depending on how possible each option is). If the aliens are pulling the trigger that probably means some Keter class object is causing, or is about to cause, some interstellar level K-class trouble might as well shutdown all our Keters and see if the aliens calm down.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ Expounding on possible spoilers.
- Expounding on possible spoilers.

I wonder then, if the Foundation is truly convinced it won't work, or in case it won't work, the Keter skips that are to be "unsealed" are just to get things over with as quickly as possible, in a manner of speaking.

- Expounding on possible spoilers.

(Also, it's Eskobar, not Eskorbar :P)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks! I am a little worried that it is too similar to some other skips that I didn't know about when I took the project (notably SCP-2004), but I'm hoping that my mostly harder sf approach to the same material is enough to distinguish this.

Also:

+ Maybe a spoiler?
- Maybe obvious

99-Tripurantaka is mostly wishful thinking and probably won't work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked this pre-rewrite, and I like it now. Dangerous and scary (for me anyway), but weird enough to freshen it up. The weird note at the end is also a marked improvement.

item.title: Acknowlegements
item.content:encoded:
This is the rewrite of the previous SCP-2631: "Cruciform Snare" by daveyoufool.

Thanks to the original author (daveyoufool), Eskobar, Agent Macleod, Petrograd and psul.

Note that this probably exists in a hard-ish sf version of the Foundation-verse without some of the more space operatic objects that aren't consistent with this (unless you want them to be!).

EDIT: This is my first rewrite, my first Keter and my first K-class scenarios, by the way. Also I think my first new MTFs.
